segments.text: As you can see, your pattern is scored based on the number 10, so out of 10 is your 1.
As you can see, it's split up into two parts, we have our accuracy and our presentation of the site.
So we'll start off with the accuracy first.
So your accuracy is actually scored from a 4, so from the number 4, you're actually marked during your presentation of your pattern.
And then with that one, any mistakes that you make, if it's a minor mistake, get a minus of a 0.1.
If it's a major mistake...
If it's a major mistake, get a minus of 0.3.
So I'll just let you guys know about the difference between the two.
So with a minor mistake, it's a very, very small thing.
So let's say we're doing an outer block.
If it's not your shoulder height, maybe it's a little bit lower, or maybe it's a tiny bit higher, that's a minor mistake.
So that's a 0.1.
A major mistake would be, you're supposed to do an outer block in the pattern, but you forget and you say you do a reach hand strike instead.
So that would be a major mistake.
So with a minor mistake, let's say pattern 1, for example, there's 20 steps, it's not uncommon to have up to maybe 14, 15 mistakes in the 20 steps.
So the reason for that one being, it needs to be absolutely perfect to not get a deduction of 0.1.
So it's not uncommon, let's say there's 20 steps, and you made...
Let's say 15 mistakes, so what would that be?
So we'll leave that there on this side, and we'll come back and we'll just calculate the score at the end.
So a major mistake, say you're supposed to turn to the left, but you turn to the right, that would be a major mistake.
So obviously we do want to try and avoid any of these major mistakes.
So after we've done our pattern, that's when we move on to the presentation side.
So the presentation, as you can see, it's from a score of 6.
This 6 is actually split up into 3 parts, so we have 1, 2, 3 here.
So with this one, for a score of 2 is your power and speed, your rhythm is also a score of 2, and your expression of energy is also scored out of 2 as well.
So we'll start off with the power and speed.
So with this one, whether it's your blocks, your kicks, your strikes, your punches, anything that you do, you need to be nice and strong.
And it has to be fast as well.
So it's one thing to have a very strong punch, or a very fast punch, but it has to be both strong and fast to get high in that category.
So the next part is your rhythm.
So the rhythm, when you do start your pattern, there is a rhythm to the pattern.
It's not just really fast all the way through, it's not slow all the way through.
There's a slow, fast, slow, fast kind of aspect to it as well.
So you do need to kind of work on your rhythm, and that's also scored out of 2.
The last bit is your expression of energy.
So what is expression of energy?
So that's basically how loud you shout, so how much intensity you have during your pattern as well.
So say you're doing your pattern really well, it's really fast and it's strong, your rhythm is really great, but you're not shouting very loud.
Or you can kind of tell, the judges can kind of tell that you're not really confident, you're a little bit nervous, so that's what we need to kind of improve on as well.
So I'll just come around to this side.
So with this one, for colour belts and black belts, it's actually scored a little bit differently.
So if you're a colour belt, you'll actually, your average, so where the judges actually mark you from, it's actually from a 1.1.
So this is your average, so say you did alright, you did average compared to everyone else, your score for 1, 2 and 3, it'll start from a 1.1.
So if you're less than average in the judges' eyes, you might go down to, say for your power and speed, you might get a 1 or even a 0.9.
If you did really great, you might even get a 1.3 or a 1.4.
So each individual category, you need to obviously get as high as you can.
If you're a black belt, generally depending on the competition, you will be scored from a 1.3.
So 1.3 would be your average that you start with.
So say your power and speed was above average, you might get scored a 1.4 for now.
Say your rhythm was only about average, it wasn't amazing, it might be just a 1.3.
But let's say you didn't shout very loud, so your expression of energy, your average was 1.3, it might drop to say, let's say 1.0.
